# Autogenerated data triage

dtk emits **302 `auto_generated` data objects** (436,554 B) that it could not attribute
to a source translation unit. All already byte-match (`fuzzy_match_percent == 100`): dtk
reconstructs every one from the user's disc at build time. But because they carry empty
`progress_categories`, they sit **outside** the scored `game`/`sdk` denominators, so they drag the
whole-DOL `matched_data_percent` down without representing real outstanding work.

This file classifies **every** auto object so each excluded byte is named, and the genuinely
recoverable backlog is scoped and pre-attributed. Companion machine-readable manifest:
[`autogen_data_triage.csv`](autogen_data_triage.csv). Regenerate both with
`python3 tools/autogen_data_triage.py` after any rebuild of `report.json`.

## Headline: it is one asset, not a wall

| class | objs | bytes | % of auto | disposition |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **Boot-logo asset** | 1 | 264,644 | 61% | out-of-scope, documented — the **only** true asset |
| **BSS (zero-init)** | 47 | 19,840 | 5% | already matches; no committed bytes; trivial to attribute |
| **Mappable data** | 254 | 152,070 | 35% | **in-scope backlog** — real structured data |
| **total** | 302 | 436,554 | 100% | |

The only byte range that can **never** be committed is the boot logo (264,644 B). Everything
else is zero-init BSS or genuine structured data. Consequently the **ceiling on whole-DOL data
match is ~78%** (`(total_data − boot_logo) / total_data`), and the in-scope
(`game`+`sdk`) data metric — which already excludes the asset by design — can reach **100%**.

## The one asset

A single 264,644-byte object, the boot / loading-screen texture set (Nintendo / Rareware /
Dolby logos, then `0xFF` padding, reused as the GX FIFO). Fully identified, extracted by dtk from
the disc, never committed — see [`embedded_assets.md`](embedded_assets.md). Its entropy is low
(~1.9) *because* it is 70% padding, so it is caught by padding-fraction, not entropy. **Done.**

## BSS — 19,840 B, 47 objs

`.bss`/`.sbss`/`.sbss2` zero-init ranges. Zero committed bytes and already matching; attributing
them (declaring the globals in the owning TU) is upside for the raw ratio with no data to write,
but low value — do last, or leave.

## Mappable backlog — 152,070 B, 254 objs

Genuine structured data dtk failed to attribute. By subtype:

| subtype | objs | bytes | what it is |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `ptr-table` | 85 | 23,064 | reloc-dense pointer / jump / vtable tables |
| `float-pool` | 66 | 40,112 | read-only float / const pools (mostly `.sdata2`) |
| `const-data` | 7 | 9,930 | small `.sdata`/`.rodata` constants |
| `string-data` | 1 | 436 | string / char tables |
| `data-table` | 95 | 78,528 | structured `.data` tables (game data, display lists, offset tables) |

**249 of 254 objects (127,686 B, 84% of mappable bytes) are
high-confidence attributed**: the blob's start address is *exactly* a known source unit's section
end, i.e. dtk split off data that continues straight out of that unit. Recovering these is "append
this data to the TU that already owns the bytes just before it," not archaeology.

Full per-object attribution is in the CSV. Confidence: `high` = exact section-end adjacency;
`med` = nearest real unit before/after; `low` = no neighbour found.

## Recommended order of attack

1. **`high`-confidence `data-table` / `ptr-table` blobs, largest first** — each appends to a named
   TU that already owns the preceding bytes; biggest raw-% gain per unit of effort.
2. **`float-pool` / `const-data`** — mechanically typed (`f32`/`f64`/const arrays), low risk.
3. **`string-data`** — small and easy, but watch SJIS / byte-wise editing rules.
4. **BSS** — optional cleanup; only global declarations to home.
5. **Boot logo** — no action; already done.

## Method (reproducible)

`tools/autogen_data_triage.py`. Per object: `powerpc-eabi-objdump -h -r -s` on the read-only target
`.o`, then classify by section + byte signals (Shannon entropy, `0xFF`-padding fraction, relocation
density, pointer-word fraction `0x80003000..0x81800000`, printable-ASCII fraction, longest string
run). Owner attribution matches each blob's start against the section-end table of all non-auto
units in `report.json`. No target object is modified.
